name.description: The name of the LLM model to use

url.description: URL to chat completions service. Currently supports OpenAI protocol (e.g. LM Studio)

prompt_token_limit.description: Prompt size limit in tokens. History messages will be removed once limit is reached.

prompt_token_target.description: Target token count when removing history messages. Setting this slightly lower than prompt_token_limit reduces the number of context history trims and can allow LLMs to optimise context processing

file_tools.description: File and folder AI tool calls

folders.description: List of folders the agent can access, exposed to the LLM as aliases.

alias.description: The name the LLM uses to refer to this folder (e.g. 'notes')

path.description: Absolute path to the folder on disk.

trash_path.description: Absolute path to the 'trash' folder, where files are moved when the LLM 'deletes' them.

storage_path.description: Path and filename to long-term memory JSON file

retrieve_memory_limit.description: Maximum number of long-term memories retrieved

working_memory_storage_path.description: Path and filename to working-memory JSON file

working_memory_limit.description: Maximum number of working memories to store

extra_info.description: Any extra information for the agent. This is included in the system prompt.

model_size.description: fast-whisper model size. Larger models are more accurate but slower. 'small' is recommended for CPU use.

trigger_key.description: pynput key name to use as the push-to-talk trigger (e.g. 'ctrl_r' for right Ctrl). Hold to record, release to transcribe.

language.description: BCP-47 language code for transcription (e.g. 'en'). Omit to enable auto-detection.

reminders.description: Reminder tool configuration. Omit to disable the reminder tools.

storage_path.description: Path to JSON file storing active reminders.

speak.description: Text-to-speech tool configuration. Omit to disable the speak tool.

enabled.description: Whether the speak tool is enabled.

storage_path.description: Path to JSON file storing active tasks.

completed_log_path.description: Path to append-only JSONL file where completed tasks are logged.

list_limit.description: Maximum number of active items on the to-do list.

email.description: Email tool configuration. Supports a read-only user inbox and a read/write agent inbox.

user_inboxes.description: One or more read-only IMAP inboxes for the user's personal email. All inboxes are merged into a single consolidated view.

name.description: Short account alias used in email IDs (e.g. "slingshot"). Must be unique. No colons.

username.description: IMAP login username (usually the email address)

password.description: IMAP password or app-specific password. If omitted, looked up from the OS keyring and the user is prompted on first run.

agent_inbox.description: IMAP/SMTP inbox for the agent. Can send email to addresses in the send_whitelist.

username.description: IMAP/SMTP login username (usually the email address)

password.description: IMAP/SMTP password or app-specific password. If omitted, the password is looked up from the OS keyring (Windows Credential Manager on Windows), and the user is prompted to enter it on first run.

send_whitelist.description: Email addresses the agent is permitted to send to.

storage_path.description: Path to JSON file used to track read emails and polling state.

max_body_chars.description: Maximum characters returned per read_email call. Larger emails are truncated with a continuation hint.

poll_interval_seconds.description: How often (in seconds) to poll inboxes for new emails.

web_search.description: Web search tool configuration using the Tavily API. Omit to disable. API key is stored in the OS keyring and prompted on first run.

enabled.description: Whether the web search tool is enabled.

allowed_apps.description: White list of apps the agent is allowed to launch.

name.description: The name of the application. This is what the agent will use to launch it.

description.description: Short description of the application, displayed to the agent when apps are listed.

start_in_folder.description: The full path to the folder to start the application in. If ommitted will be inferred from the 'path'.
